beats.activity: Wake up, coffee by the philodendrons, check weather and emails.
beats.reasoning: Sage is a night owl but still maintains a morning ritual; the warm Friday forecast appeals to his mood-driven planning, so he's up earlier to maximize daylight on site visits.

beats.activity: Drive to Tukwila office, listening to music or a podcast.
beats.reasoning: Standard commute from Wedgwood to Tukwila; early start lets him tackle site-plan review before the day heats up.

beats.activity: Review planting specs and site documents for Volunteer Park; coordinate with contractors.
beats.reasoning: Three hours of focused office work on the ongoing Volunteer Park project; warm weather ahead means he'll want to get out to the site during the cooler morning hours.

beats.activity: Drive to Volunteer Park site to inspect native plantings and soil conditions.
beats.reasoning: Mid-morning site visit while it's still reasonably cool; Sage values hands-on time seeing the work take shape, and Friday afternoon heat will be climbing.

beats.activity: Walk the Volunteer Park site, assess plantings, check soil moisture and drainage.
beats.reasoning: Extended on-site inspection; the warm, breezy weather is ideal for observing how the landscape is settling. This is where Sage feels most grounded and purposeful.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a nearby Capitol Hill spot, light and refreshing given the heat.
beats.reasoning: Quick meal near the site; the warm weather pushes Sage toward something cool and light rather than heavy. Molly Moon's fits the neighborhood and his laid-back style.

beats.activity: Drive back to Tukwila office to wrap up the day's specs and file updates.
beats.reasoning: Return commute; heat is now in full effect, so being indoors in air conditioning makes sense. Afternoon is typically when Sage handles administrative closeout.

beats.activity: Finalize site specifications, respond to emails, prepare for next week's contractor meetings.
beats.reasoning: Final work block before heading home; Sage wraps up the week's loose ends in the cool office, avoiding the peak afternoon heat outside.

beats.activity: Drive home to Wedgwood, stop at Ace Hardware for houseplant supplies.
beats.reasoning: Friday errand on the way home; Sage regularly picks up supplies for his plants. Ballard location is roughly on route from Tukwila to Wedgwood, and he enjoys this ritual.

beats.activity: Arrive home, water plants, tend to the philodendrons and others, settle in.
beats.reasoning: Back home early enough to enjoy the warm evening light on the porch before the Capitol Hill Pride street closures make downtown increasingly crowded and inaccessible.

beats.activity: Paint on the porch while the light holds; listen to the neighborhood settle into evening.; Light dinner, tidy up, relax indoors with a board game or reading; wind down for the night.
beats.reasoning: Sage's favorite wind-down ritual; the warm, breezy Friday and golden hour light are perfect for painting. Capitol Hill Pride crowds are building downtown, but he's content at home observing from a distance.
Seven-and-a-half hours of sleep, ending at 6am; Sage is a night owl so this bedtime is slightly later than typical, and the overnight span shows a full sleep cycle.

diary:
Woke to a perfect Friday—77 degrees and that soft Seattle breeze that makes you remember why you chose this place. Coffee by the philodendrons, as always. The drive to Tukwila felt shorter than usual; I was thinking about the Volunteer Park specs the whole way.

Got to the site around mid-morning and walked it myself. The native plantings are establishing beautifully—the soil moisture is holding well after last week's rain. Grabbed a light lunch at Molly Moon's on Capitol Hill; the ice cream was cold and necessary. Heading back to the office, I could already feel the Pride energy building downtown. Broadway was getting cordoned off, people buzzing with anticipation.

Back home by mid-afternoon. Watered everything, picked up some fresh potting soil at Ace. Spent the evening painting on the porch while the light lasted—just abstract stuff, nothing serious. Listened to the neighborhood shift into weekend mode. There's something about a warm Friday that makes you want to move slowly.
thought_process: I chose to focus on the site work this morning because the specs have been nagging at me all week—wanted to see the plantings myself rather than just trust the reports. The drive to Capitol Hill made me aware of the Pride preparations, but I didn't feel pulled into it; I'm happy for the energy, but I'm also content in my own rhythms. The decision to paint on the porch instead of heading downtown was about honoring the light and the quiet I needed after a solid week. The board game got bumped for painting—I wasn't in a social mood, just wanted to make something with my hands and wind down naturally.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
